body.title: Move safety gates out of env-hydration path — file-only, never graph
body.result: Shipped in 7d1d5ec9. SAFETY_GATE_NAMES skip-list excludes IGOR_TIER5_ENABLED / IGOR_ARBITER_ENABLED / IGOR_SELF_EDIT_ENABLED from env_sync push and hydrate. Defense-in-depth: hydrate refuses with WARNING log if a gate ever shows up in the graph. File system is the only source of truth — graph-poisoning path closed. 6 tests.
body.description:
Pass-2 Area 4 P1-8.1 CONFIRMED_WORSE — highest-stakes finding in Area 4. Three safety flags (IGOR_TIER5_ENABLED, IGOR_ARBITER_ENABLED, IGOR_SELF_EDIT_ENABLED) were round-tripping through the config graph: env_sync.push_vars_to_graph stored them in SYSCFG_* nodes; hydrate_from_graph pulled them back into os.environ on boot. Any engram with cortex.store() access to a SYSCFG_ node could flip them invisibly (no file write needed).

Fix: SAFETY_GATE_NAMES skip-list excludes these from push AND hydrate. Defense-in-depth warning logs if a safety-gate node ever appears in the graph. File system is the only source of truth.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/env_sync.py (SAFETY_GATE_NAMES constant + push/hydrate guards), tests/test_env_sync_safety_gates.py (new).
**Design rules:** docs-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** IN — skip-list + push/hydrate exclusions + warning log. OUT — changing what the flags gate; adding new gates; altering file-load path.
**Test plan:** 6 tests: name constants, _is_safety_gate helper, push_vars_to_graph excludes all three gates, hydrate refuses to set gates found in graph (logs warning), hydrate still applies non-gate vars unchanged.
